title: Add Portfolio domain model with rebalance and ownership logic
author: eval-bot

context_units.diff_patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+from dataclasses import dataclass, field
+from typing import List
+
+from fastapi import HTTPException
+from repositories.portfolio_repo import PortfolioRepository
+
+
+@dataclass
+class Holding:
+    ticker: str
+    shares: float
+    avg_cost: float
+
+
+@dataclass
+class Portfolio:
+    owner_id: str
+    holdings: List[Holding] = field(default_factory=list)
+
+    def total_value(self, prices: dict[str, float]) -> float:
+        return sum(h.shares * prices.get(h.ticker, 0.0) for h in self.holdings)
+
+    def rebalance(self, target_weights: dict[str, float]) -> list[dict]:
+        repo = PortfolioRepository()
+        current = repo.get_current_allocations(self.owner_id)
+        trades = []
+        for ticker, weight in target_weights.items():
+            current_pct = current.get(ticker, 0.0)
+            if abs(current_pct - weight) > 0.01:
+                trades.append({"ticker": ticker, "action": "rebalance", "target": weight})
+        return trades
+
+    def assert_owner(self, requesting_user_id: str) -> None:
+        if self.owner_id != requesting_user_id:
+            raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Not your portfolio")

@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@

+from dataclasses import dataclass, field
+from typing import List
+
+from fastapi import HTTPException

+
+
+@dataclass

+    ticker: str
+    shares: float
+    avg_cost: float
+
+
+@dataclass
+class Portfolio:
+    owner_id: str
+    holdings: List[Holding] = field(default_factory=list)
+
+    def total_value(self, prices: dict[str, float]) -> float:

+
+    def rebalance(self, target_weights: dict[str, float]) -> list[dict]:
+        repo = PortfolioRepository()
+        current = repo.get_current_allocations(self.owner_id)
+        trades = []
+        for ticker, weight in target_weights.items():
+            current_pct = current.get(ticker, 0.0)
+            if abs(current_pct - weight) > 0.01:
+                trades.append({"ticker": ticker, "action": "rebalance", "target": weight})
+        return trades

expected_findings.title: Framework type (FastAPI HTTPException) leaks into the domain model
expected_findings.rationale: Importing HTTPException into a domain model couples business logic to the FastAPI HTTP layer. The domain should raise its own exceptions (e.g. PermissionError) and the API layer should translate them to HTTP responses.

expected_findings.title: Domain model imports from the repository layer — illegal dependency direction
expected_findings.rationale: domain/ must not import from repositories/. Repositories depend on domain types, not the reverse. This creates a circular import and prevents testing the domain in isolation.
